description: RULE: empty screen. A bare screen node with no children and no anchor. Proves the A: prefix line is always present even with an empty anchor and an empty body.
RULE: basic login. header + textfield(email) + textfield(password) + button, all with stable ids and a route anchor. The canonical realistic screen all other collision vectors mutate.

RULE: locale-invariance (text excluded). Structurally identical to the basic login with identical ids; Node has no text field, so an English vs Japanese render of the same screen is literally the same tree and MUST hash identically to the basic-login vector.

RULE: transient-drop (spinner). The basic login plus a trailing spinner/progress node. The spinner is dropped, so this MUST hash identically to the basic-login vector (a loading state is the same node as the loaded state).

RULE: transient-drop (snackbar + transient error banner). The basic login plus a snackbar and a transient-flagged error group (with a child that also goes away). Both are dropped, so this MUST hash identically to the basic-login vector (error showing == error hidden).
RULE: repeated-collapse (3 items). A list with 3 identical listitem subtrees collapses to one *-marked token with the count dropped.

RULE: repeated-collapse (5 items, count dropped). Same list as the 3-item vector but with 5 identical listitems. Because the count is dropped on collapse, this MUST hash identically to the 3-item vector (the dynamic-content explosion is gone).

RULE: collision-fix via input type. Same skeleton as the basic login but the second textfield is type=text instead of type=password. The input type is a discriminator, so this MUST differ from the basic-login vector.
RULE: collision-fix via icon. Same skeleton as the basic login but the submit button carries an icon. The icon is a discriminator, so this MUST differ from the basic-login vector.
ANCHOR: same route + same structure -> same. Settings screen with a header + a switch row, route /settings. Pairs with the next vector to prove route is part of identity.

ANCHOR: different route + same structure -> different. Byte-identical structure to the /settings vector but route /profile. Proves the Settings-vs-Profile collision is resolved by the anchor prefix (MUST differ from the /settings vector).

ANCHOR: same route + different structure -> different. Route /settings (same as the /settings vector) but the structure is a wizard step 2 (a different child set). Proves a multi-step flow at one route splits into distinct nodes (MUST differ from the /settings vector).
ANCHOR: parameterized route (item 42). Route template /item/:id rendered for id 42, with a detail structure. The anchor stores the TEMPLATE, not the id. Pairs with the next vector.

ANCHOR: parameterized route (item 99) -> same template, same node. Same route template /item/:id (the concrete id is NOT in the anchor) and same structure as the item-42 vector. MUST hash identically to the item-42 vector (one node for any id).

RULE: unknown role normalizes to node + non-consecutive identical siblings are NOT collapsed. A group containing button, an unknown 'carousel' role (-> node), then button again: the two buttons are not adjacent so no *-marker appears, and the carousel serializes as node.

VALUE: empty value-class. A textfield value node with an empty value -> V:key:amount=EMPTY. Pairs with the ZERO and POS1 vectors to prove the value-class folds into the canonical signature for value-role nodes.
VALUE: zero value-class. Same textfield as the EMPTY vector but value '0' -> V:key:amount=ZERO. MUST differ from the EMPTY and POS1 vectors (three distinct states).
VALUE: POS1 value-class. Same textfield as the EMPTY vector but value '3' (a single-digit positive) -> V:key:amount=POS1. MUST differ from the EMPTY and ZERO vectors.

VALUE: numeric counter at 0. A status value node (status is a value-role; not in the structural vocabulary, so the body token is node) with value '0' -> V:key:count=ZERO. Pairs with the counter-at-5 vector.
VALUE: numeric counter at 5. Same status node as the counter-at-0 vector but value '5' -> V:key:count=POS1. MUST differ from the counter-at-0 vector (ZERO vs POS1).

VALUE: chrome label with text is NOT value-bearing. A header (chrome role) carrying a value field; chrome roles never enter the V: section, so this hashes IDENTICALLY to the same tree with no value at all (backward-compatible, no V: section emitted).

VALUE: grouped/locale number is locale-safe -> NONEMPTY. A textfield value '1,234' uses a grouping separator; the oracle does NOT guess locale formats, so it classifies to NONEMPTY (not POSL) -> V:key:amount=NONEMPTY. MUST differ from the period-decimal numeric vectors.

VALUE: two different POS1 values bucket the same (3). A status node with value '3'. Pairs with the value-'7' vector: both fall in the POS1 bucket so they MUST hash identically (the value-class is bounded, not the raw value).
VALUE: two different POS1 values bucket the same (7). A status node with value '7'. MUST hash identically to the value-'3' POS1 vector (both bucket to POS1).
